source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Crane/Other Lifting Device

event.top_event: Gas migration during cementing with well control incident
actions_taken: Well shut in and flow diverted through Diverter
Non-essential personnel evacuated from platform and rig
8 personnel evacuated from platform, 54 from rig
Personnel transferred by rig crane to workboat K-Marine VI
Annular closed to stop flow to rig floor
Fluid directed out of port side diverter line
Annulus filled with 9.8ppg water based mud
Cement bond log run to inspect cement integrity
event.summary: On October 23, 2014, while performing drilling operations on the C-22 well, the Ensco 87 drilling rig experienced a well control incident during cementing operations. A gas bubble from the formation entered the well during the cement job and migrated up the well before the cement had time to cure. At approximately 10:15am, fluid began flowing from the casing valve at 15 barrels per hour. The crew cleared the well and closed the Diverter Annular at 11:00am, after which fluid discharged to a height of approximately 20 feet above the rig floor. For approximately 30 minutes, the well intermittently discharged a mixture of gas, mud, and cement from the Diverter line. The Emergency Shut Down was initiated and 62 personnel were evacuated. No injuries, equipment damage, or pollution were sustained.

threats.name: Gas migration into well during cement cure
Loss of well control during casing annulus cleanup

consequences.name: Uncontrolled discharge of gas, mud, and cement to atmosphere
Personnel evacuation from platform and rig

controls.name: Casing valve monitoring by drill crew

supporting_text: A member of the drill crew had been positioned at the casing valve to monitor the well for flow
At approximately 10:15am, the employee noticed fluid start to flow out of the casing valve at a rate of approximately 15 barrels per hour

controls.name: Diverter Annular closure
controls.barrier_role: Containment and safe discharge of well fluids

supporting_text: Unable to close the Diverter Annular with the grout string still in the hole, the crew rushed to clear the well
As the Annular moved into the closed position, the fluid being discharged from the well reached a height of approximately 20 feet above the rig floor
Once the Annular was fully closed, flow to the rig floor was stopped and the fluid was directed out of the port side diverter line

controls.name: Emergency Shut Down (ESD) system
controls.barrier_role: Facility-wide emergency response and personnel protection

supporting_text: The platform's Emergency Shut Down (ESD) was initiated and all non-essential personnel were evacuated from both the platform and the rig
In total, 8 personnel were evacuated from the platform and 54 personnel evacuated from the rig

controls.name: Cement job planning and execution
controls.barrier_role: Prevent gas migration by ensuring adequate cement coverage

supporting_text: 7,469 cubic feet of class H cement was pumped into the well to secure the casing
The crew observed 450 barrels of cement returns at the surface, confirming sufficient coverage of the cement
While the rig was waiting for the cement to harden, the gas was able to migrate up the well and into the atmosphere
it was determined that the event was due to a gas bubble that had entered the well during the cement job
A cement bond log was run to inspect the integrity of the cement in the well
The results of the log showed good coverage of the cement in the well

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
